mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the realtime web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for SurrealDB.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the 'surrealdb' namespace using the specified chart version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace surrealdb

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

```

> Create a namespace for SurrealDB.
> Install SurrealDB with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the SurrealDB pods are running correctly in the 'surrealdb' namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> This command lists all pods in the surrealdb namespace.

steps.title: Create an example database
steps.description:
This step creates a sample database to verify that SurrealDB is functioning as expected.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n surrealdb -l app=surrealdb -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n surrealdb -- surrealdb create db mydb

```

> Replace 'mydb' with your desired database name.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n surrealdb -l app=surrealdb -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n surrealdb -- surrealdb create db mydb

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step will remove SurrealDB and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surrealdb

kubectl delete namespace surrealdb

```

> Uninstall the SurrealDB release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n surrealdb -l app=surrealdb -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n surrealdb -- surrealdb backup mydb

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> Backup your database before upgrading.
> Upgrade SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n surrealdb -l app=surrealdb -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n surrealdb -- surrealdb backup mydb

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified database does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the database name is correct or create the database if it doesn't exist.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service is not running or not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule SurrealDB pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits on your cluster or scale down other workloads.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or firewall rules are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check network policies and ensure that the necessary ports are open.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new query syntax and improved performance for concurrent connections.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb'.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n surrealdb -l app=surrealdb -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n surrealdb -- surrealdb create db mydb

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
